skill.name: foundations-market-intelligence
skill.description: Market analysis and competitive intelligence for startup teams evaluating opportunities, segments, competitors, timing, and go-to-market choices.
skill.summary: Create structured startup market intelligence reports with TAM/SAM/SOM, segment profiles, competitor analysis, and opportunity scoring.
skill.author: BellaBe

security_audit.summary: AI review found no executable code, shell commands, prompt injection, secret access, or data exfiltration behavior in the reviewed skill. The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, market-sizing acronyms, and ordinary business analysis terms.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings occur on Markdown template fences and example output blocks. No shell command, interpreter call, or executable script is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown fences, field names, or example report content. I found no Ruby backticks, shell syntax, or command execution instructions.

low_findings.title: Market Acronym SAM Misclassified as Sensitive Windows File
low_findings.description: The sensitive-file findings match the acronym SAM in TAM/SAM/SOM market sizing. In this context, SAM means Serviceable Addressable Market, not the Windows Security Account Manager database.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location uses SAM as a market-sizing term alongside TAM and SOM. No evidence found of Windows file access, credential extraction, or filesystem instructions.

low_findings.title: Business Terms Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings occur in prose, headings, placeholder rows, and market analysis examples. No cryptographic function, hashing algorithm, encryption API, or security implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are ordinary skill metadata, market templates, or business advice. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or related weak cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Business Context
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings occur in customer persona, common pitfalls, and market validation guidance. They do not request host inspection, network discovery, credential discovery, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines describe market research structure and segment validation. No evidence found of commands or instructions to enumerate the user's machine or network.

content.value_statement: Startup teams often lack a consistent way to size markets, compare competitors, and choose target segments. This skill turns market inputs into structured intelligence for strategy, positioning, and validation planning.
actual_capabilities: Structures TAM, SAM, and SOM analysis using top-down, bottom-up, and value theory methods.
Builds competitor matrices with positioning, strengths, weaknesses, pricing, funding, and strategic threats.
Profiles customer segments, ideal customer profiles, buyer roles, objections, and acquisition entry points.
Assesses market dynamics, growth stage, disruption signals, category strategy, and timing readiness.
Combines market, competition, and execution fit into a go or no-go opportunity score.
Produces recommended validation actions and assumptions to test next.
limitations: It depends on user-provided market facts or separately gathered research sources.
It does not verify live market data unless the host tool provides browsing or research access.
Market size outputs are estimates and should be validated with primary research.
It does not replace legal, financial, or investment due diligence.

use_cases.title: Evaluate a New Startup Idea
use_cases.description: Estimate market size, identify attractive customer segments, map competitors, and decide which assumptions to validate first.

use_cases.title: Plan a Market Expansion
use_cases.description: Compare geographies, segments, timing, and competitive pressure before entering an adjacent market.

use_cases.title: Refresh Investor Materials
use_cases.description: Turn market research into clear TAM/SAM/SOM, competitive positioning, and opportunity scoring for a fundraising narrative.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Market Scan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a product idea and need a first market view.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the market for this product idea: [product idea]. Geography: [target geography]. Industry: [industry vertical]. Include TAM/SAM/SOM, top segments, competitors, and next validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Landscape Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when positioning and differentiation are the main questions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a competitive intelligence report for [product or category]. Compare the top direct and indirect competitors, their positioning, pricing, strengths, gaps, and likely responses.

prompt_templates.title: Segment Prioritization
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when choosing the first customer segment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Profile and rank customer segments for [product idea]. Score each segment by size, pain severity, urgency, accessibility, willingness to pay, and evidence quality.

prompt_templates.title: Opportunity Go No-Go
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when deciding whether to enter, pivot, or pause.
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize market size, customer evidence, competitors, timing, and execution fit for [opportunity]. Give an opportunity score, go/no-go recommendation, risks, assumptions, and three de-risking actions.

output_examples.input: Analyze AI personal finance coaching for Gen Z in the United States.
output_examples.output: A market intelligence report with TAM/SAM/SOM ranges, Gen Z segment profiles, a competitor matrix, timing assessment, and three validation actions.

output_examples.input: Compare B2C skincare recommendations with a B2B beauty retail analytics pivot.
output_examples.output: A side-by-side opportunity assessment covering customer segments, willingness to pay, competitive intensity, execution fit, and a recommended path.

output_examples.input: Refresh our market view six months after launch with strong enterprise traction.
output_examples.output: An updated market report focused on new competitors, validated or invalidated assumptions, segment performance, expansion options, and revised next actions.

best_practices: Provide geography, customer type, business model, and any known competitors before asking for analysis.
Ask the skill to separate evidence from assumptions so weak inputs stay visible.
Use the recommended validation actions before treating market estimates as facts.
anti_patterns: Do not use TAM as a revenue forecast without SAM, SOM, and adoption constraints.
Do not rely only on top-down industry reports when bottom-up customer math is possible.
Do not ask for a final strategy recommendation without giving execution constraints.

faq.question: What inputs does this skill need?
faq.answer: Provide a product idea, target geography, industry vertical, and any assumptions about customers, competitors, or constraints.

faq.question: Can it calculate TAM, SAM, and SOM?
faq.answer: Yes. It structures market sizing with multiple methods and asks for conservative assumptions where evidence is weak.

faq.question: Does it gather live market data?
faq.answer: Not by itself. It can use research you provide, or live browsing if the host environment supports it.

faq.question: Can it help choose a first customer segment?
faq.answer: Yes. It ranks segments by size, urgency, accessibility, pain severity, and willingness to pay.

faq.question: Is it suitable for investor materials?
faq.answer: Yes, but estimates should be sourced and validated before inclusion in fundraising documents.

faq.question: Can it compare a pivot against the current market?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare market attractiveness, competitive intensity, execution fit, and risks across options.
